engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is checking out a physical nightlife venue — the decision is driven by event photos, party galleries, ticket prices, lodge layouts, hours and directions. Browsing the place IS the decision.

goal.one_action: Get visitors to see upcoming events and come to the club (or reserve a lodge / buy entry), with every reservation happening off-site via Facebook

structure.archetype_note: Venue microsite: home hero + event carousel + social feeds, plus dedicated contact, reservation (tickets + lodges), gallery, FAQ pages. No native forms — all reservations/sign-ups route to Facebook.

mapped.observed_as: Imprezy w Kwadratowej w każdy piątek i sobotę
Zarezerwuj lożę na imprezę / Dostępne loże

unmapped.observed: Najbliższe wydarzenia — dated event carousel pulled from Facebook events, each linking to an external FB event page
unmapped.why_no_fit: No canonical section expresses a dated, externally-fed schedule of upcoming events/shows

unmapped.observed: Dostępne loże — lodge/room layout gallery reserved via Facebook private message
unmapped.why_no_fit: It is venue-inventory browsing (rooms) leading to an off-site reservation, not a portfolio or pricing section

proposed_new.name: event-listings
proposed_new.evidence: Home carousel 'Najbliższe wydarzenia' is a dated schedule of upcoming parties fed from Facebook events, each CTA ('Zobacz wydarzenia', 'lista FB') redirecting to the external FB event/ticket page

confidence.why: fetch_quality is full; all pages, sections, CTAs, prices and embeds are captured, and the venue/redirect pattern is unambiguous

gap_findings: event-listings (dated event schedule feeding external ticketing/Facebook) has no canonical section or closed-vocab capability
Venue-inventory browsing (bookable lodges/rooms with layout photos) reserved off-site via Facebook message is not expressible — sits between gallery, pricing, and external-booking-redirect

Entire conversion path (tickets, lodge reservation, free-entry lists) is delegated to Facebook events + private message with zero on-site forms; 'redirect to social' is the real mechanism but under-specified vs a store/booking redirect
